text: The Hour has drawn near and the moon was split ňĻin twoňļ. <sup foot_note=1134>1134</sup>

foot_notes.1134: The Meccan pagans challenged the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) to have the moon split in two if he wanted them to believe in him. The moon was split, and then re-joined, as reported by several eyewitnesses, but still the pagans refused to believe, calling this miracle ‚Äúsheer magic.‚ÄĚ

text: Yet, whenever they see a sign, they turn away, <sup foot_note=1135>1135</sup> saying, ‚ÄúSame old magic!‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.1135: A miracle (usually referred to as a ‚Äėsign‚Äô in the Quran) can be defined as an extraordinary event manifesting divine intervention in human affairs. Every prophet was given miracles to prove he was commissioned by Allah. Moses ( Ô∑ļ ) was given the miracle of the staff and many others. Jesus ( Ô∑ļ ) gave life to the dead and healed the blind and the leper. And MuŠł•ammad ( Ô∑ļ ) received the Quran, a literary miracle, to challenge the masters of Arabic eloquence. The Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) performed some other miracles such as splitting the moon, multiplying food and water, and healing some of his companions.

text: They rejected ňĻthe truthňļ and followed their own desires‚ÄĒand every matter will be settled‚ÄĒ
even though the stories ňĻof destroyed nationsňļ that have already come to them are a sufficient deterrent.
ňĻThis Quran isňļ profound ňĻinňļ wisdom, but warnings are of no benefit ňĻto themňļ.
So turn away from them ňĻO Prophetňļ. ňĻAnd wait forňļ the Day ňĻwhenňļ the caller <sup foot_note=1136>1136</sup> will summon ňĻthemňļ for something horrifying. <sup foot_note=1137>1137</sup>

foot_notes.1136: Angel Isr√Ęf√ģl will blow the Trumpet, causing all to be raised from the dead for judgment.

text: With eyes downcast, they will come forth from the graves as if they were swarming locusts,
rushing towards the caller. The disbelievers will cry, ‚ÄúThis is a difficult Day!‚ÄĚ
Before them, the people of Noah denied ňĻthe truthňļ and rejected Our servant, calling ňĻhimňļ insane. And he was intimidated.
So he cried out to his Lord, ‚ÄúI am helpless, so help ňĻmeňļ!‚ÄĚ
So We opened the gates of the sky with pouring rain,
and caused the earth to burst with springs, so the waters met for a fate already set.
We carried him on that ňĻArk madeňļ of planks and nails,
sailing under Our ňĻwatchfulňļ Eyes‚ÄĒa ňĻfairňļ punishment on behalf of the one ňĻtheyňļ denied.
We certainly left this <sup foot_note=1138>1138</sup> as a sign. So is there anyone who will be mindful?
Then how ňĻdreadfulňļ were My punishment and warnings!
And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful?

foot_notes.1139: lit., a bitter and screaming wind.

text: that snatched people up, leaving them like trunks of uprooted palm trees.
Then how ňĻdreadfulňļ were My punishment and warnings!
And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful?

arguing, ‚ÄúHow can we follow one ňĻaverageňļ human being from among us? We would then truly be misguided and insane.
Has the revelation been sent down ňĻonlyňļ to him out of ňĻall ofňļ us? In fact, he is a boastful liar.‚ÄĚ
ňĻIt was revealed to ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł•,ňļ ‚ÄúThey will soon know who the boastful liar is.
We are sending the she-camel as a test for them. So watch them ňĻcloselyňļ, and have patience.
And tell them that the ňĻdrinkingňļ water must be divided between them ňĻand herňļ, each taking a turn to drink ňĻevery other dayňļ.‚ÄĚ
But they roused a companion of theirs, so he dared to kill ňĻherňļ.

The people of Lot ňĻalsoňļ rejected the warnings.
We unleashed upon them a storm of stones. As for ňĻthe believers ofňļ Lot‚Äôs family, We delivered them before dawn
as a blessing from Us. This is how We reward whoever gives thanks.

And they even demanded his angel-guests from him, <sup foot_note=1140>1140</sup> so We blinded their eyes. ňĻAnd they were told,ňļ ‚ÄúTaste then My punishment and warnings!‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.1140: Who came in the form of handsome men.

text: And indeed, by the early morning they were overwhelmed by an unrelenting torment.
ňĻAgain they were told,ňļ ‚ÄúTaste now My punishment and warnings!‚ÄĚ
And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful?

ňĻButňļ they rejected all of Our signs, so We seized them with the ňĻcrushingňļ grip of the Almighty, Most Powerful.

ňĻSoonňļ their united front will be defeated and ňĻforced toňļ flee. <sup foot_note=1141>1141</sup>

text: Better yet, the Hour is their appointed time‚ÄĒand the Hour will be most catastrophic and most bitter.
Indeed, the wicked are ňĻentrenchedňļ in misguidance, and ňĻare bound forňļ blazes.
On the Day they will be dragged into the Fire on their faces, ňĻthey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúTaste the touch of Hell!‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=1142>1142</sup>

foot_notes.1142: Saqar is one of the names of Hell.

text: Indeed, We have created everything, perfectly preordained.
Our command <sup foot_note=1143>1143</sup> is but a single word, <sup foot_note=1144>1144</sup> done in the blink of an eye.
We have already destroyed the likes of you. So will any ňĻof youňļ be mindful?
Everything they have done is ňĻlistedňļ in ňĻtheirňļ records.
Every matter, small and large, is written ňĻpreciselyňļ.
Indeed, the righteous will be amid Gardens and rivers, <sup foot_note=1145>1145</sup>

foot_notes.1145: Rivers of water, milk, honey, and wine. See 47:15.

text: at the Seat of Honour in the presence of the Most Powerful Sovereign.
